Name: (R)-3-hydroxybutyrate
Definition: The conjugate base of (R)-3-hydroxybutyric acid.

Name: 2-hydroxyestrone
Definition: A 2-hydroxy steroid that is estrone substituted by a hydroxy group at position 2.

Name: oleamide
Definition: A fatty amide derived from oleic acid.

Name: N-carbamoyl-beta-alaninate
Definition: A monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of N-carbamoyl-β-alanine arising from deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: 2'-deoxynucleoside 3'-monophosphate(2-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any 2'-deoxynucleoside 3'-monophosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: coproporphyrin III(4-)
Definition: A cyclic tetrapyrrole anion obtained by deprotonation of the four carboxy groups of coproporphyrin III; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: oxo-ETE anion
Definition: An icosanoid anion arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function of any oxoicosatetraenoic acid.

Name: hydrogen acceptor
Definition: A molecular entity that can undergo reduction by the gain of hydrogen atom(s).

Name: (14S,15R)-EET(1-)
Definition: A 14,15-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (14S,15R)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (14R,15S)-EET(1-)
Definition: A 14,15-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (14R,15S)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (11S,12R)-EET(1-)
Definition: An 11,12-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (11S,12R)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: ribonucleoside 3'-monophosphate(2-)
Definition: A ribonucleoside 3'-monophosphate(2−) oxoanion in which both of the acidic hydroxy groups from the phosphate moiety are deprotonated.

Name: (11R,12S)-EET(1-)
Definition: An 11,12-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (11R,12S)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (8S,9R)-EET(1-)
Definition: An 8,9-EET(1−) that is the conjugate base of (8S,9R)-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: tauroursodeoxycholate
Definition: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of tauroursodeoxycholic acid arising from deprotonation of the sulfonate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: glycoursodeoxycholate
Definition: A N-acylglycinate that is the conjugate base of glycoursodeoxycholic acid. obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-tetracosanoyltaurine(1-)
Definition: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate obtained by deprotonation of the sulfonate group of N-tetracosanoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 18(S)-HEPE(1-)
Definition: An 18-HEPE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 18(S)-HEPE, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 15(S)-HEPE(1-)
Definition: A HEPE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 15(S)-HEPE, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: O(3)-(N-acetyl-beta-D-galactosaminyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucuronosyl-(1->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-xylosyl)-L-serine(1-) residue
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the glucuronosyl carboxy group of O3-(N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl-(1→4)-β-D-glucuronosyl-(1→3)-β-D-galactosyl-(1→3)-β-D-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-D-xylosyl)-L-serine residue.

Name: 1,4-benzoquinones
Definition: Any member of the class of benzoquinones that is 1,4-benzoquinone or its C-substituted derivatives.

Name: hepoxilin A3(1-)
Definition: A hepoxilin anion that is the conjugate base of hepoxilin A<small><sub>3</sub></small>, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: bradykinin(2+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group and protonation of the amino and guanidino groups of bradykinin; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: pregnenolone sulfate(1-)
Definition: A steroid sulfate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of pregnenolone sulfate, obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: androsterone sulfate(1-)
Definition: A steroid sulfate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of androsterone sulfate, obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: prostaglandin G1(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin G<small><sub>1</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: prostaglandin G3(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin G<small><sub>3</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: prostaglandin H3(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin H<small><sub>3</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: Phe-Arg(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy terminus and protonation of the side-chain guanidino group and the amino terminus of Phe-Arg; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-acetylglycyl residue
Definition: An N-acyl-α-aminoacyl group derived from N-acetylglycine

Name: N-acetyl-L-valyl residue
Definition: An N-acyl-α-aminoacyl group derived from N-acetyl-L-valine.

Name: N-acetyl-L-cysteinyl residue
Definition: An N-acyl-α-aminoacyl group derived from N-acetyl-L-cysteine.

Name: N-acetyl-L-threonyl residue
Definition: An N-acyl-α-aminoacyl group derived from N-acetyl-L-threonine.

Name: dopamine 3-O-sulfate zwitterion
Definition: A zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the sulfonyl to the amino group of dopamine 3-O-sulfate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: dopamine 4-O-sulfate zwitterion
Definition: A zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the sulfonyl to the amino group of dopamine 4-O-sulfate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 13-HODE(1-)
Definition: A HODE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 13-HODE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 9-HODE(1-)
Definition: A HODE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 9-HODE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: lithocholate sulfate(2-)
Definition: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxylic acid and sulfate functions of lithocholic acid sulfate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-oleoylglycinate
Definition: An N-acylglycinate resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoylglycine. It is believed to be an intermediate in oleamide biosynthesis. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-oleoyl-L-phenylalaninate
Definition: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-phenylalanine. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-arachidonoyl-L-phenylalaninate
Definition: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-arachidonoyl-L-phenylalanine. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-oleoyl-L-serinate
Definition: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-serine. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-oleoyl-L-glutaminate
Definition: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-glutamine. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-oleoyl-L-leucinate
Definition: An N-acyl-L-α-amino acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of N-oleoyl-L-leucine. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: ergothioneine(1+)
Definition: An α-amino-acid cation obtained by protonation of the imidazole ring of ergothioneine. It is the major microspecies at pH 7.3 (according to Marvin v 6.2.0.).

Name: N-[omega-(9R,10R)-epoxy-(13R)-hydroxy-(11E)-octadecenoyloxy]acyl-beta-D-glucosyl-(1<->1)-sphingosine
Definition: A β-D-glucosylceramide in which a β-D-glucosyl residue is attached to the primary hydroxyl group of N-[ω-(9R,10R)-epoxy-(13R)-hydroxy-(11E)-octadecadienoyloxy]acyl-sphing-4E-enine.

Name: 14,15-epoxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z)-icosatrienoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 14,15-epoxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z)-icosatrienoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5-hydroxy-(6E,8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 5-hydroxy-(6E,8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 12-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,10E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 12-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,10E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 15-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of 15-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5'-end 2'-deoxyribonucleotide(2-) residue
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any 5'-end 2'-deoxyribonucleotide residue; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5'-end 2'-deoxyribonucleoside residue
Definition: A nucleoside residue derived from any 2'-deoxyribonucleoside having the linkage point at position 3'.

Name: alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosaminide(1-)
Definition: A carbohydrate acid derivative anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of any α-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2→3)-β-D-galactosyl-(1→4)-N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminide; major species ar pH 7.3.

Name: (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 24-sulfate(1-)
Definition: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 24-sulfate.

Name: (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3-sulfate(1-)
Definition: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3-sulfate.

Name: (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3,24-disulfate(2-)
Definition: A steroid sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo groups of (24S)-hydroxycholesterol 3,24-disulfate.

Name: 13(R)-HODE(1-)
Definition: A 13-HODE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 13(R)-HODE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 19-hydroxy-5alpha-dihydrotestosterone
Definition: An androstanoid that is 5α-dihydrotestosterone carrying an additional hydroxy susbstituent at position 19.

Name: 19-oxo-5alpha-dihydrotestosterone
Definition: An androstanoid that is 5α-dihydrotestosterone carrying an additional oxo susbstituent at position 19.

Name: acetoacetate
Definition: A 3-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of acetoacetic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: 17beta-hydroxy-5alpha-estr-1(10)-en-3-one
Definition: A 3-oxo-5α-steroid that is 5α-estrane which is substituted by an oxo group at position 3, a hydroxy group at the 17β position, and which has a double bond between positions 1 and 10. It is a 19-norsteroid arising from successive oxidation of dihydrotestosterone at C19.

Name: myristamide
Definition: A fatty amide of myristic acid.

Name: 2-[(11R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,12E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (11R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,12E,14Z)-icosatetraenoyl.

Name: 2-[(15R)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine

Name: 2-[(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-icosatetraenoyl.

Name: (25R)-26-hydroxycholestanol
Definition: A cholestanoid that is cholestanol which is substituted by a hydroxy group at position 26 and has R-configuration at position 25.

Name: arachidonoyl amine
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of arachidonic acid with ammonia.

Name: N(6)-[(E)-but-2-enoyl]-L-lysine residue

Name: (5Z,8S,9S,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate

Name: (5Z,8Z,11Z,14R,15R)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate

Name: (5Z,8Z,11R,12R,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate

Name: (5Z,8Z,11S,12S,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
Definition: A (5Z,8Z,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of (5Z,8Z,11S,12S,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (5Z,8Z,11Z,14S,15S)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate

Name: (5Z,8R,9R,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate

Name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion

Name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroperoxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3- phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as (9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroperoxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl.

Name: 2-[(9S,11R)-epidioxy-(15S)-hydroxy-(5Z,13E)-prostadienoyl]-sn-glycero-3- phosphocholine
Definition: A prostanoid-LPC that is the ammonium betaine of a 2-(prostaglandin H2)-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine arising from deprotonation of the phosphate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3'-end 3'-(N-acyl-L-alpha-aminoacyl)adenylyl(1-) residue
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any 3'-end 3'-(N-acyl-L-α-aminoacyl)adenylyl residue; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-diheptanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A phosphatidylcholine 14:0 in which both acyl groups are specified as heptanoyl.

Name: 2-heptanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as heptanoyl.

Name: 5'-end GTP-ribonucleotide(5-) residue
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and triphosphate OH groups of any 5'-end GTP-ribonucleotide residue; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5'-end ribonucleoside residue
Definition: A nucleoside residue derived from any ribonucleoside having the linkage point at position 3'.

Name: O(3)-(beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-GalNAc-(1->4)-beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->4)-beta-D-Xyl)-L-serine(2-) residue

Name: O(3)-(poly[beta-D-GalNAc-(1->4)-beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)]-beta-D-GalNAc-(1->4)-beta-D-GlcA-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->3)-beta-D-Gal-(1->4)-beta-D-Xyl)-L-serine polyanionic residue

Name: all-trans-retinyl heptanoate
Definition: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of heptanoic acid with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol.

Name: (S,S)-bis-(3-oleoylglycero)-1-phosphate(1-)
Definition: A 3,3'-lysobisphosphatidate obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of (S,S)-bis-(3-oleoylglycero)-1-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-(4-methoxyphenyl)-N-methyl-N-[(3-methyloxetan-3-yl)methyl]methanamine(1+)
Definition: An ammonium ion obtained by protonation of the amino group of 1-(4-methoxyphenyl)-N-methyl-N-[(3-methyloxetan-3-yl)methyl]methanamine. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 2-{[(4-methoxybenzyl)(methyl)amino]methyl}-2-methylpropane-1,3-diol(1+)
Definition: An ammonium ion resulting from the protonation of the amino group of 2-{[(4-methoxybenzyl)(methyl)amino]methyl}-2-methylpropane-1,3-diol.

Name: [8)-alpha-Neu5Ac-(2->]n polyanion
Definition: A polyanionic polymer obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy groups of [8)-α-Neu5Ac-(2→]n; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-acyl-(4E,14Z)-sphingadienine
Definition: A ceramide obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of any fatty acid with the amino group of (4E,14Z)-sphingadienine.

Name: 2-octanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as octanoyl.

Name: all-trans-retinyl octanoate
Definition: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of octanoic acid with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol.

Name: 2-dodecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group is specified as dodecanoyl.

Name: all-trans-retinyl dodecanoate
Definition: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of dodecanoic (lauric) acid with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol.

Name: alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->3)-beta-D-galactoside(1-)

Name: phenyl sulfate oxoanion
Definition: An aryl sulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of any phenyl sulfate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: poly(nucleoside 5'-monophosphate) polyanion
Definition: An ionic polymer obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any poly(nucleoside 5'-monophosphate).

Name: ethanediol derivative
Definition: A substituted ethane carrying two hydroxy groups.

Name: S-(ADP-D-ribosyl)-L-cysteine(2-) residue
Definition: An L-α-amino acid residue derived from S-(ADP-D-ribosyl)-L-cysteine.

Name: 3-sulfinatopyruvate(2-)
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion  obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy and sulfino groups of 3-sulfinylpyruvic acid. Major microspecies at pH 7.3.

Name: 4-nitrophenyl sulfate
Definition: An aryl sulfate oxoanion resulting from the deprotonation of the sulfooxy group of 4-nitrophenyl hydrogen sulfate. The major microspecies at pH 7.3.

Name: O-acetyl-L-serine residue
Definition: An L-α-amino acid residue derived from O-acetyl-L-serine.

Name: (5S)-5-hydroxy-L-lysinium residue

Name: R-H
Definition: Any organic molecular entity that contains at least one activated carbon-hydrogen (C-H) bond. R can be any aliphatic or aromatic group.

Name: C-terminal O-(ADP-D-ribosyl)-glycine(2-) residue
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the diphosphate OH groups of C-terminal O-(ADP-D-ribosyl)-glycine residue.

Name: (9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadecatrienamide

Name: 2'-3'-cGAMP(2-)

Name: N-palmitoyl-sphingoid base
Definition: A ceramide where the N-acyl is a palmitoyl chain but the sphingoid base is not defined

Name: (8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosatrienamide
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of all-cis-icosa-8,11,14-trienoic acid with ammonia.

Name: (11Z,14Z,17Z)-icosatrienamide
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of all-cis-icosa-11,14,17-trienoic acid with ammonia.

Name: (11Z,14Z)-icosadienamide
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of (11Z,14Z)-icosadienoic acid with ammonia.

Name: nervonamide
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of (15Z)-tetracosenoic acid with ammonia.

Name: gondamide
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of (11Z)-icos-11-enoic acid with ammonia.

Name: petroselinamide
Definition: A primary fatty amide resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of petroselinic acid with ammonia.

Name: N-(docosanoyl)ethanolamine
Definition: An N-(long-chain-acyl)ethanolamine that is the ethanolamide of docosanoic acid.

Name: N-(15Z-tetracosenoyl)ethanolamine
Definition: A N-(long-chain-acyl)ethanolamine that is the ethanolamide of (15Z)-tetracosenoic acid.

Name: N-oleoyltaurine(1-)
Definition: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate obtained by deprotonation of the sulfonate group of N-oleoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-docosanoyltaurine(1-)
Definition: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate(1−) obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of N-docosanoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-nervonoyltaurine(1-)
Definition: A fatty acid-taurine conjugate obtained by deprotonation of the sulfonate group of N-nervonoyltaurine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: angiotensin I dizwitterion
Definition: A peptide zwitterion that is the dizwitterionic form of angiotensin I having both carboxy groups deprotonated and the aspartyl amino group and arginine side-chain protonated. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

Name: [des-Phe(8), des-Arg(9)]-bradykinin(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group and protonation of the primary amino and guanidino groups of [des-Phe<small><sup>8</small></sup>, des-Arg<small><sup>9</small></sup>]-bradykinin. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

Name: neurotensin(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained from the deprotonation of the carboxy groups of <small>L</small>-α-glutamyl and <small>L</small>-leucine residues, and protonation of the side chains of <small>L</small>-lysyl and <small>L</small>-arginyl residues of neurotensin. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

Name: His-Leu zwitterion
Definition: A dipeptide zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino terminus of His-Leu. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: S-nitroso-L-cysteine residue
Definition: An L-α-amino-acid residue derived from S-nitroso-L-cysteine.

Name: N-arachidonoyl-L-serine(1-)
Definition: A N-(fatty acyl)-L-serine(1−) that is the conjugate base of N-arachidonoyl-L-serine, resulting from the derotonation of the carboxy group. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A 1-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the amino to the phosphate group of 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine.

Name: 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A phosphatidylethanolamine 37:5 zwitterion in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as 10Z-heptadecenoyl and arachidonoyl respectively.

Name: 1-(10Z-heptadecenoyl)-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A phosphatidylethanolamine 35:3 zwitterion in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as 10Z-heptadecenoyl and linoleoyl respectively.

Name: retinal
Definition: An enal that consists of 3,7-dimethyl-9-nona-2,4,6,8-tetraenal (double bond geometry unspecified) carrying a 2,6,6-trimethylcyclohex-1-en-1-yl group at the 9-position.

Name: choline
Definition: A choline that is the parent compound of the cholines class, consisting of ethanolamine having three methyl substituents attached to the amino function.

Name: acetylcholine
Definition: Actylcholine is an ester of acetic acid and choline, which acts as a neurotransmitter.

Name: pyruvate
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of pyruvic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: water
Definition: An oxygen hydride consisting of an oxygen atom that is covalently bonded to two hydrogen atoms

Name: hydron
Definition: The general name for the hydrogen nucleus, to be used without regard to the hydrogen nuclear mass (either for hydrogen in its natural abundance or where it is not desired to distinguish between the isotopes).

Name: D-threo-isocitrate(3-)
Definition: The D-threo-form of isocitrate(3−).

Name: (R)-malate(2-)
Definition: An optically active form of malate having (R)-configuration.

Name: (S)-malate(2-)
Definition: An optically active form of malate having (S)-configuration.

Name: formate
Definition: A monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of formic acid. Induces severe metabolic acidosis and ocular injury in human subjects.

Name: malonate(2-)
Definition: A dicarboxylic acid dianion obtained by the deprotonation of the carboxy groups of malonic acid.

Name: (R)-2-hydroxyglutarate(2-)
Definition: A 2-hydroxyglutarate(2−) that has (2R)-configuration.

Name: 5,6-dihydrouracil
Definition: A pyrimidine obtained by formal addition of hydrogen across the 5,6-position of uracil.

Name: 5alpha-androstane-3,17-dione
Definition: The 5α-stereoisomer of androstane-3,17-dione.

Name: dihydroxyacetone
Definition: A ketotriose consisting of acetone bearing hydroxy substituents at positions 1 and 3. The simplest member of the class of ketoses and the parent of the class of glycerones.

Name: androsterone
Definition: An androstanoid that is 5α-androstane having a hydroxy substituent at the 3α-position and an oxo group at the 17-position. It is a metabolite of dehydroepiandrosterone .

Name: halide anion
Definition: A monoatomic monoanion resulting from the addition of an electron to any halogen atom.

Name: 5beta-cholestan-3-one
Definition: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid that is 5β-cholestane substituted by an oxo group at position 3.

Name: cholesterol
Definition: A cholestanoid consisting of cholestane having a double bond at the 5,6-position as well as a 3β-hydroxy group.

Name: cholest-4-en-3-one
Definition: A cholestanoid that is cholest-4-ene substituted by an oxo group at position 3.

Name: urea
Definition: A carbonyl group with two C-bound amine groups. The commercially available fertilizer has an analysis of 46-0-0 (N-P2O5-K2O).

Name: guanine
Definition: A 2-aminopurine carrying a 6-oxo substituent.

Name: hydrogen peroxide
Definition: An inorganic peroxide consisting of two hydroxy groups joined by a covalent oxygen-oxygen single bond.

Name: 17beta-hydroxy-5alpha-androstan-3-one
Definition: A 17β-hydroxy steroid that is testosterone in which the 4,5 double bond has been reduced to a single bond with α-configuration at position 5.

Name: (R)-carnitine
Definition: The (R)-enantiomer of carnitine.

Name: ubiquinones
Definition: Any benzoquinone derived from 2,3-dimethoxy-5-methylbenzoquinone; one of a group of naturally occurring homologues. The redox-active quinoid moiety usually carries a polyprenoid side chain at position 6, the number of isoprenoid units in which is species-specific. Ubiquinones are involved in the control of mitochondrial electron transport, and are also potent anti-oxidants.

Name: androst-4-ene-3,17-dione
Definition: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid that is androst-4-ene substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 17. It is a steroid hormone synthesized in the adrenal glands and gonads.

Name: phenylacetaldehyde
Definition: An aldehyde that consists of acetaldehyde bearing a methyl substituent; the parent member of the phenylacetaldehyde class of compounds.

Name: oxaloacetate(2-)
Definition: A C4-dicarboxylate resuting from deprotonation of both carboxy groups of oxaloacetic acid.

Name: 17beta-estradiol
Definition: The 17β-isomer of estradiol.

Name: lanosterol
Definition: A tetracyclic triterpenoid that is lanosta-8,24-diene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at the 3β position. It is the compound from which all steroids are derived.

Name: carbon dioxide
Definition: A one-carbon compound with formula CO<small><sub>2</sub></small> in which the carbon is attached to each oxygen atom by a double bond. A colourless, odourless gas under normal conditions, it is produced during respiration by all animals, fungi and microorganisms that depend directly or indirectly on living or decaying plants for food.

Name: alpha,alpha-trehalose
Definition: A trehalose in which both glucose residues have α-configuration at the anomeric carbon.

Name: pregnenolone
Definition: A 20-oxo steroid that is pregn-5-ene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3 and an oxo group at position 20.

Name: 5alpha-cholest-8-en-3beta-ol
Definition: A cholestanoid that is 5α-cholestane substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3.

Name: (S)-lactate
Definition: An optically active form of lactate having (S)-configuration.

Name: 4-methylthio-2-oxobutanoate
Definition: The 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion derived from 4-methylthio-2-oxobutanoic acid. The major microspecies at pH 7.3, it is formed from <small>L</small>-methionine via the action of methionine transaminase.

Name: 2-oxobutanoate
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-oxobutanoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: (S)-2-hydroxyglutarate(2-)
Definition: A 2-hydroxyglutarate(2−) that has (2S)-configuration.

Name: 2-oxoglutarate(2-)
Definition: An oxo dicarboxylate obtained by deprotonation of both carboxy groups of 2-oxoglutaric acid.

Name: corticosterone
Definition: A 21-hydroxy steroid that consists of pregn-4-ene substituted by hydroxy groups at positions 11 and 21 and oxo groups at positions 3 and 20. Corticosterone is a 21-carbon steroid hormone of the corticosteroid type produced in the cortex of the adrenal glands.

Name: formaldehyde
Definition: An aldehyde resulting from the formal oxidation of methanol.

Name: choline alfoscerate
Definition: A member of the class of phosphocholines that is the choline ester of sn-glycero-3-phosphate. It is one of the major osmolyte in the renal medullary cells.

Name: citrate(3-)
Definition: A tricarboxylic acid trianion, obtained by deprotonation of the three carboxy groups of citric acid.

Name: cortisone
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is pregn-4-ene substituted by hydroxy groups at positions 17 and 21 and oxo group at positions 3, 11 and 20.

Name: 11-deoxycorticosterone
Definition: A mineralocorticoid that is progesterone substituted at position 21 by a hydroxy group.

Name: 5beta-androstane-3,17-dione
Definition: An androstane-3,17-dione with a 5β-configuration.

Name: cholesteryl ester
Definition: A sterol ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of any carboxylic acid with the 3-hydroxy group of cholesterol.

Name: progesterone
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid hormone in which a pregnane skeleton carries oxo substituents at positions 3 and 20 and is unsaturated at C(4)-C(5). As a hormone, it is involved in the female menstrual cycle, pregnancy and embryogenesis of humans and other species.

Name: hexanoate
Definition: A short-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of hexanoic acid (also known as caproic acid).

Name: nicotinamide
Definition: A pyridinecarboxamide that is pyridine in which the hydrogen at position 3 is replaced by a carboxamide group.

Name: benzaldehyde
Definition: An arenecarbaldehyde that consists of benzene bearing a single formyl substituent; the simplest aromatic aldehyde and parent of the class of benzaldehydes.

Name: 4-methylumbelliferone
Definition: A hydroxycoumarin that is umbelliferone substituted by a methyl group at position 4.

Name: estrone
Definition: A 17-oxo steroid that is estra-1,3,5(10)-triene substituted by an hydroxy group at position 3 and an oxo group at position 17.

Name: myo-inositol
Definition: An inositol having myo- configuration.

Name: aldehydo-N-acyl-D-glucosamine
Definition: The open-chain form of an N-acyl-D-glucosamine.

Name: all-trans-retinol
Definition: A retinol in which all four exocyclic double bonds have E- (trans-) geometry.

Name: testosterone
Definition: An androstanoid having 17β-hydroxy and 3-oxo groups, together with unsaturation at C-4‒C-5..

Name: D-glyceraldehyde
Definition: The <small>D</small>-enantiomer of glyceraldehyde.

Name: 2-monoglyceride
Definition: A monoglyceride in which the acyl substituent is located at position 2.

Name: aldehyde
Definition: A compound RC(=O)H, in which a carbonyl group is bonded to one hydrogen atom and to one R group.

Name: enol-oxaloacetate
Definition: A C4-dicarboxylate resulting from the removal of a proton from both carboxylic acid groups of enol-oxaloacetic acid.

Name: hydrogen donor
Definition: A molecular entity that can undergo oxidation by the loss of hydrogen atom(s).

Name: hydrogencarbonate
Definition: The carbon oxoanion resulting from the removal of a proton from carbonic acid.

Name: uracil
Definition: A common and naturally occurring pyrimidine nucleobase in which the pyrimidine ring is substituted with two oxo groups at positions 2 and 4. Found in RNA, it base pairs with adenine and replaces thymine during DNA transcription.

Name: trans-hexadec-2-enal
Definition: A hexadecenal containing a double bond at position 2 (the trans-stereoisomer).

Name: all-trans-retinyl palmitate
Definition: An all-trans-retinyl ester obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of palmitic (hexadecanoic acid) with the hydroxy group of all-trans-retinol. It is used in cosmetic products to treat various skin disorders such as acne, skin aging, wrinkles, dark spots, and also protect against psoriasis.

Name: nitrate
Definition: A nitrogen oxoanion formed by loss of a proton from nitric acid. Principal species present at pH 7.3.

Name: 3-(indol-3-yl)pyruvate
Definition: A indol-3-yl carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 3-(indol-3-yl)pyruvic acid

Name: cortisol
Definition: A 17α-hydroxy-C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is pregn-4-ene substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 20 and hydroxy groups at positions 11, 17 and 21. Cortisol is a corticosteroid hormone or glucocorticoid produced by zona fasciculata of the adrenal cortex, which is a part of the adrenal gland. It is usually referred to as the "stress hormone" as it is involved in response to stress and anxiety, controlled by corticotropin-releasing hormone (CRH). It increases blood pressure and blood sugar, and reduces immune responses.

Name: L-thyroxine sulfate(1-)
Definition: A thyroxine sulfate(1−) that has <small>L</small>-configuration. The major microspecies at pH 7.3.

Name: 9H-xanthine
Definition: An oxopurine in which the purine ring is substituted by oxo groups at positions 2 and 6 and N-9 is protonated.

Name: desmosterol
Definition: A cholestanoid that is cholesta-5,24-diene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3. It is an intermediate metabolite obtained during the synthesis of cholesterol.

Name: glycine betaine
Definition: The amino acid betaine derived from glycine.

Name: glycerol
Definition: A triol with a structure of propane substituted at positions 1, 2 and 3 by hydroxy groups.

Name: 4-methylumbelliferyl acetate
Definition: An acetate ester consiting of umbelliferone carrying a 7-O-acetyl group.

Name: methanol
Definition: The primary alcohol that is the simplest aliphatic alcohol, comprising a methyl and an alcohol group.

Name: organohalogen compound
Definition: A compound containing at least one carbon-halogen bond (where X is a halogen atom).

Name: thymine
Definition: A pyrimidine nucleobase that is uracil in which the hydrogen at position 5 is replaced by a methyl group.

Name: methylarsonous acid
Definition: A one-carbon compound that is arsonous acid in which the hydrogen attached to arsenic is replaced by a methyl group.

Name: triglyceride
Definition: Any glyceride resulting from the condensation of all three hydroxy groups of glycerol (propane-1,2,3-triol) with fatty acids.

Name: 4-methyl-2-oxopentanoate
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 4-methyl-2-oxopentanoic acid.

Name: all-trans-retinal
Definition: A retinal in which all four exocyclic double bonds have E- (trans-) geometry.

Name: 7alpha-hydroxycholest-4-en-3-one
Definition: A cholestanoid consisting of a cholesterol core having an oxo group at the 3-position, a C=C bond at the 4,5-position and an α-hydroxy group at the 7-position.

Name: calcidiol
Definition: A hydroxycalciol that is calciol in which the hydrogen at position 25 has been replaced by a hydroxy group. A prehormone resulting from the oxidation of calciol in the liver, it is further hydroxylated in the kidney to give calcitriol, the active form of vitamin D<small><sub>3</sub></small>.

Name: butyrate
Definition: A short-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of butyric acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: chloride
Definition: A halide anion formed when chlorine picks up an electron to form an an anion.

Name: keto-phenylpyruvate
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion resulting from deprotonation of the carboxy group of keto-phenylpyruvic acid.

Name: thiocyanate
Definition: A pseudohalide anion obtained by deprotonation of the thiol group of thiocyanic acid.

Name: diglyceride
Definition: A glyceride that is glycerol in which any two of the hydroxy groups have been acylated. In the structure shown, two of the R groups (positions not specified) are acyl groups while the remaining R group can be either H or an alkyl group.

Name: phylloquinone
Definition: A member of the class of phylloquinones that consists of 1,4-naphthoquinone having methyl and phytyl groups at positions 2 and 3 respectively. The parent of the class of phylloquinones.

Name: 17,21-dihydroxy-5beta-pregnane-3,11,20-trione
Definition: A 4,5-dihydrocortisone that has β- configuration at position 5.

Name: (R)-3,4-dihydroxymandelaldehyde
Definition: A 3,4-dihydroxymandelaldehyde that has R-configuration.

Name: 3-phosphonatooxypyruvate(3-)
Definition: A carboxyalkyl phosphate oxoanion resuting from deprotonation of the carboxy and phosphate groups of 3-phosphooxypyruvic acid.

Name: dodecanoate
Definition: A medium-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of dodecanoic acid (lauric acid); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: alpha-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosylceramide
Definition: A glycotriaosylceramide having α-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-<small>D</small>-glucose as the trisaccharide component.

Name: N-acyl-beta-D-galactosylsphingosine
Definition: An N-acyl-D-galactosylsphingosine in which the anomeric configuration of the galactosyl residue is β; sphingosine substituted at the O-1 position by a β-D-galactosyl group and at the N-2 position by an acyl group.

Name: Met-enkephalin zwitterion
Definition: A peptide zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino terminus of Met-enkephalin. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: Arg-Phe(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy terminus and protonation of the side-chain guanidino group and the amino terminus of Arg-Phe; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: Leu-enkephalin zwitterion
Definition: A peptide zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino terminus of Leu-enkephalin. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: substance P(3+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained from the protonation of the amino and guanidino groups of substance P; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: L-Phe-L-Phe-Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation that is the conjugate acid of L-Phe-L-Phe-Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2, arising from the protonation of the N-terminal amino group. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation that is the conjugate acid of Gly-L-Leu-L-Met-NH2, arising from the protonation of the N-terminal amino group. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: L-Leu-L-Met-NH2(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation that is the conjugate acid of L-Leu-L-Met-NH2, arising from the protonation of the N-terminal amino group. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: goralatide(1-)
Definition: A peptide anion obtained by removal of protons from the two carboxy groups as well as protonation of the primary amino group of goralatide; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-acetyl-L-seryl-L-aspartate(2-)
Definition: A peptide anion obtained by deprotonation of the two carboxy groups of N-acetyl-L-seryl-L-aspartic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: Lys-Pro(1+)
Definition: A peptide cation obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy terminus and protonation of the side-chain amino group and the amino terminus of Lys-Pro; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: Ile-Leu zwitterion
Definition: A dipeptide zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino terminus of Ile-Leu.

Name: Tyr-Gly-Gly zwitterion
Definition: A tripeptide zwitterion resulting from the transfer of a proton from the carboxy group to the amino group of Tyr-Gly-Gly. Major microspecies at pH 7.3.

Name: Phe-Met zwitterion
Definition: A dipeptide zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino terminus of Phe-Met.

Name: Phe-Leu zwitterion
Definition: A dipeptide zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino terminus of Phe-Leu.

Name: 17-oxo steroid
Definition: Any  oxo steroid carrying the oxo group at position 17.

Name: all-trans-13,14-dihydroretinoate
Definition: A retinoid anion that is the conjugate base of all-trans-13,14-dihydroretinoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 15-deoxy-Delta(12,14)-prostaglandin J2-S-glutathione conjugate(2-)
Definition: A prostanoid anion obtained by deprotonation of the three carboxy groups and protonation of the glutamyl α-amino group of 15-deoxy-Δ12,14-prostaglandin J2-S-glutathione conjugate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1D-myo-inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate(6-)
Definition: Hexaanion of 1D-myo-inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate arising from glabal deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5beta-dihydrotestosterone
Definition: A 17β-hydroxyandrostan-3-one that has β- configuration at position 5. It is a metabolite of testosterone.

Name: 2-phenylethanaminium
Definition: The cation obtained by protonation of the amino group of 2-phenylethylamine.

Name: benzylaminium
Definition: The conjugate acid of benzylamine; major product at pH 7.3.

Name: beta-D-glucosyl-N-acylsphingosine
Definition: A D-glucosyl-N-acylsphingosine in which the glucosyl head group has β-configuration at the anomeric centre.

Name: 7alpha,12alpha-dihydroxy-5beta-cholestan-3-one
Definition: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid that is 5β-cholestan-3-one bearing two additional hydroxy substituents at positions 7α and 12α.

Name: primary methyl ammonium ion
Definition: An ammonium ion derivative resulting from the protonation of the nitrogen atom of a primary methyl amino compound. Major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 7alpha-hydroxy-5beta-cholestan-3-one
Definition: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid that is 5β-cholestan-3-one bearing an additional hydroxy substituent at position 7α .

Name: N(6)-[(S)-lactoyl]-L-lysine residue
Definition: An N6-acyl-L-lysine residue that has (S)-lactoyl as the N6-acyl group.

Name: ribonucleotidyl-2'-3'-cyclophospho-GMP(2-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any ribonucleotidyl-2'-3'-cyclophospho-GMP; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5'-end 5'-hydroxy ribonucleotidyl-ribonucleotide(1-)
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any 5'-hydroxy-ribonucleotidyl-ribonucleotide residue.

Name: 5'-end 5'-hydroxy-deoxyribonucleotidyl-deoxyribonucleotide(1-) residue
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any 5'-end 5'-hydroxy-deoxyribonucleotidyl-deoxyribonucleotide residue; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (R,S)-glycero-3-phospho-(3'-acyl-1'-glycerol)(1-)
Definition: An anionic phospholipid obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of any (R,S)-glycero-3-phospho-(3'-acyl-1'-glycerol); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (R,S)-glycero-1-phospho-(3'-(9Z-octadecenoyl)-1'-glycerol)(1-)
Definition: An anionic phospholipid obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group of (R,S)-glycero-1-phospho-(3'-(9Z-octadecenoyl)-1'-glycerol); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 11-oxo-5alpha-dihydrotestosterone
Definition: An androstanoid that is 5α-dihydrotestosterone carrying an additional oxo substituent at position 11.

Name: 11-keto-5alpha-androstane-3alpha,17beta-diol
Definition: An androstanoid that is 5α-androstane carrying an oxo group at position 11 and two hydroxy groups at the 3α and 17β positions.

Name: behenate
Definition: A straight-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of behenic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

Name: hydroquinones
Definition: Benzenediols that have the hydroxy substituents in the 1- and 4-positions.

Name: adrenosterone
Definition: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid that is androst-4-ene carrying three oxo-substituents at positions 3, 11 and 17.

Name: octadecanoate
Definition: A fatty acid anion 18:0 that is the conjugate base of octadecanoic acid (stearic acid). Stearates have a variety of uses in the pharmaceutical industry.

Name: octanoate
Definition: A straight-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of octanoic acid (caprylic acid); believed to block adipogenesis.

Name: anandamide
Definition: An N-acylethanolamine 20:4 resulting from the formal condensation of carboxy group of arachidonic acid with the amino group of ethanolamine.

Name: 5,6-dihydrothymine
Definition: A pyrimidone obtained by formal addition of hydrogen across the 5,6-position of thymine.

Name: aldosterone
Definition: A pregnane-based steroidal hormone produced by the outer-section (zona glomerulosa) of the adrenal cortex in the adrenal gland, and acts on the distal tubules and collecting ducts of the kidney to cause the conservation of sodium, secretion of potassium, increased water retention, and increased blood pressure. The overall effect of aldosterone is to increase reabsorption of ions and water in the kidney.

Name: decanoate
Definition: A fatty acid anion 10:0 that is the conjugate base of decanoic acid.

Name: 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetaldehyde
Definition: A phenylacetaldehyde in which the 3 and 4 positions of the phenyl group are substituted by hydroxy groups.

Name: 24,25-dihydrolanosterol
Definition: A 3β-sterol formed from lanosterol by reduction across the C-24‒C-25 double bond.

Name: 1-O-oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: An oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group at position 1 is (9Z)-octadecenoyl (oleoyl) and the hydroxy group at position 2 is unsubstituted.

Name: 2-oxopentanoate
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-oxopentanoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: dehydroepiandrosterone
Definition: An androstanoid that is androst-5-ene substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3 and an oxo group at position 17. It is a naturally occurring steroid hormone produced by the adrenal glands.

Name: fatty acid anion
Definition: The conjugate base of a fatty acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group of the corresponding fatty acid.

Name: ammonium
Definition: An onium cation obtained by protonation of ammonia.

Name: calciol
Definition: A hydroxy seco-steroid that is (5Z,7E)-9,10-secocholesta-5,7,10(19)-triene in which the pro-S hydrogen at position 3 has been replaced by a hydroxy group. It is the inactive form of vitamin D3, being hydroxylated in the liver to calcidiol (25-hydroxyvitamin D3), which is then further hydroxylated in the kidney to give calcitriol (1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3), the active hormone.

Name: 5alpha-pregnane-3,20-dione
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid hormone that is 5α-pregnane substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 20. It is a metabolite of progestrone.

Name: manganese(2+)
Definition: A divalent metal cation in which the metal is manganese.

Name: copper(2+)
Definition: An ion of copper carrying a double positive charge.

Name: carboxylic acid anion
Definition: The conjugate base formed when the carboxy group of a carboxylic acid is deprotonated.

Name: sodium(1+)
Definition: A monoatomic monocation obtained from sodium.

Name: potassium(1+)
Definition: A monoatomic monocation obtained from potassium.

Name: thiol
Definition: An organosulfur compound in which a thiol group, ‒SH, is attached to a carbon atom of any aliphatic or aromatic moiety.

Name: lithocholate
Definition: A bile acid anion that is the conjugate base of lithocholic acid.

Name: glycocholate
Definition: A cholanic acid conjugate anion that is the conjugate base of glycocholic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: cholate
Definition: A bile acid anion that is the conjugate base of cholic acid.

Name: fumarate(2-)
Definition: A C4-dicarboxylate that is the E-isomer of but-2-enedioate(2−)

Name: L-glutamate(1-)
Definition: An α-amino-acid anion that is the conjugate base of <small>L</small>-glutamic acid, having anionic carboxy groups and a cationic amino group

Name: D-glutamate(1-)
Definition: An α-amino-acid anion that is the conjugate base of <small>D</small>-glutamic acid, having anionic carboxy groups and a cationic amino group

Name: D-aspartate(1-)
Definition: An aspartate(1−) that is the conjugate base of <small>D</small>-aspartic acid.

Name: L-aspartate(1-)
Definition: An aspartate(1−) that is the  conjugate base of <small>L</small>-aspartic acid.

Name: L-serine residue
Definition: A serine residue that has <small>L</small> configuration.

Name: succinate(2-)
Definition: A dicarboxylic acid dianion resulting from the removal of a proton from both of the carboxy groups of succinic acid.

Name: acetate
Definition: A monocarboxylic acid anion resulting from the removal of a proton from the carboxy group of acetic acid.

Name: 5beta-pregnane-3,20-dione
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is 5β-pregnane with oxo groups at positions 3 and 20.

Name: linoleate
Definition: An octadecadienoate with cis- double bonds at the 9- and 12- positions; the conjugate base of linoleic acid.

Name: heme
Definition: A heme is any tetrapyrrolic chelate of iron.

Name: ATP(4-)
Definition: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of ATP; major species present at pH 7.3.

Name: maleate(2-)
Definition: A C4-dicarboxylate that is the Z-isomer of but-2-enedioate(2−)

Name: tetradecanoate
Definition: A long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of myristic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: oleate
Definition: A C<small><sub>18</sub></small>, long straight-chain monounsaturated fatty acid anion; and the conjugate base of oleic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

Name: elaidate
Definition: A long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of elaidic acid; shown to exert detrimental effects on mitochondrial lipid composition and function.

Name: alcohol
Definition: A compound in which a hydroxy group, ‒OH, is attached to a saturated carbon atom.

Name: 1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A glycerophosphocholine that consists of <small>L</small>-α-glycerophosphocholine carrying an unspecified alkyl substituent at position 1.

Name: tetracosanoate
Definition: A straight-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of tetracosanoic acid (lignoceric acid), formed by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: N-acylsphinganine
Definition: A ceramide consisting of sphinganine in which one of the amino hydrogens is substituted by a fatty acyl group.

Name: icosanoate
Definition: A long-chain fatty acid anion resulting from the removal of a proton from the carboxy group of icosanoic acid (arachidic acid).

Name: palmitoleate
Definition: A hexadec-9-enoate that is the conjugate base of palmitoleic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: petroselinate
Definition: An unsaturated fatty acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of petroselinic acid. The major species at pH 7.3.

Name: alpha-linolenate
Definition: A linolenate that is the conjugate base of α-linolenic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

Name: (15Z)-tetracosenoate
Definition: A tetracosenoate that is the conjugate base of nervonic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

Name: arachidonate
Definition: A long-chain fatty acid anion resulting from the removal of a proton from the carboxy group of arachidonic acid.

Name: gondoate
Definition: A very long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of gondoic acid, formed by deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

Name: L-lysinium(1+)
Definition: An optically active form of lysinium having <small>L</small>-configuration.

Name: L-argininium(1+)
Definition: The <small>L</small>-enantiomer of argininium(1+).

Name: tyraminium
Definition: An ammonium ion that is the conjugate acid of tyramine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: epoxide
Definition: Any cyclic ether in which the oxygen atom forms part of a 3-membered ring.

Name: beta-D-fructofuranose 1,6-bisphosphate(4-)
Definition: A <small>D</small>-fructofuranose 1,6-bisphosphate(4−) that is the conjugate base of β-<small>D</small>-fructofuranose 1,6-bisphosphate.

Name: diphosphate(3-)
Definition: A trivalent inorganic anion obtained by removal of all three protons from diphosphoric acid.

Name: carboxylic ester
Definition: An ester of a carboxylic acid, R<small><sup>1</small></sup>C(=O)OR<small><sup>2</small></sup>, where R<small><sup>1</small></sup> = H or organyl and R<small><sup>2</small></sup> = organyl.

Name: L-serine zwitterion
Definition: A serine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of <small>L</small>-serine.

Name: phenols
Definition: Organic aromatic compounds having one or more hydroxy groups attached to a benzene or other arene ring.

Name: 1-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1-acyl-sn-glycerol where arachidonoyl forms the 1-acyl group.

Name: 11-oxotestosterone
Definition: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid that is testosterone carrying an additional oxo substituent at position 11.

Name: (24S)-24-hydroxycholesterol
Definition: A 24-hydroxycholesterol that has S configuration at position 24. It is the major metabolic breakdown product of cholesterol in the brain.

Name: boldenone
Definition: An  3-oxo-Δ<small><sup>1</small></sup>,Δ<small><sup>4</small></sup>-steroid substituted by an oxo group at position 3 and a β-hydroxy group at position 17. It is an  anabolic androgenic steroid that has been developed for veterinary use.

Name: tributyrin
Definition: A triglyceride obtained by formal acylation of the three hydroxy groups of glycerol by butyric acid.

Name: serotonin(1+)
Definition: An ammonium ion that is the conjugate acid of serotonin; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 2-oxohexanoate
Definition: A medium-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-oxohexanoic acid.

Name: D-serine zwitterion
Definition: A serine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of <small>D</small>-serine.

Name: acylcholine
Definition: A choline ester formed from choline and a carboxylic acid.

Name: all-trans-retinoate
Definition: A retinoate that is the conjugate base of all-trans-retinoic acid.

Name: 17beta-hydroxy steroid
Definition: A 17-hydroxy steroid in which the hydroxy group at position 17 has a β-configuration.

Name: organic disulfide
Definition: Compounds of structure RSSR in which R and R' are organic groups.

Name: 1-monoglyceride
Definition: A monoglyceride in which the acyl substituent is located at position 1.

Name: crotonate
Definition: The conjugate base of crotonic acid; used by some bacterial species as a carbon and energy source.

Name: peroxol
Definition: Monosubstitution products of hydrogen peroxide HOOH, having the skeleton ROOH, in which R is any organyl group.

Name: chenodeoxycholate
Definition: Conjugate base of chenodeoxycholic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)pyruvate
Definition: A 2-oxo monocarboxylic acid anion obtained by removal of a proton from the carboxylic acid group of 3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)pyruvic acid.

Name: glycochenodeoxycholate
Definition: A N-acylglycinate that is the conjugate base of glycochenodeoxycholic acid.

Name: taurocholate
Definition: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of taurocholic acid.

Name: taurodeoxycholate
Definition: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of taurodeoxycholic acid.

Name: galactosylceramide
Definition: Any of the cerebrosides in which the monosaccharide head group is galactose.

Name: glyoxylate
Definition: The conjugate base of glyoxylic acid.

Name: 2-acetyl-1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acyl-1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine where the 2-O-acyl group is specified as acetyl; a potent phospholipid activator and mediator of many leukocyte functions, including platelet aggregation, inflammation, and anaphylaxis.

Name: 5alpha-androstane-3alpha,17beta-diol
Definition: The 5α-stereoisomer of androstane-3α,17β-diol.

Name: 3beta-hydroxy steroid
Definition: A 3-hydroxy steroid in which the 3-hydroxy substituent is in the  β-position.

Name: phytanate
Definition: A branched-chain saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of phytanic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group.

Name: CTP(4-)
Definition: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of CTP; major species present at pH 7.3.

Name: GTP(4-)
Definition: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of GTP; major species present at pH 7.3.

Name: dTTP(4-)
Definition: A 2'-deoxyribonucleoside 5'-triphosphate(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the four triphosphate OH groups of dTTP; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: thiamine(1+) monophosphate(2-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of thiamine(1+) monophosphate(1−), arising from the deprotonation of the phosphate group. Major microspecies at pH 7.3.

Name: D-fructofuranose
Definition: A fructofuranose that has <small>D</small> configuration.

Name: L-ascorbate
Definition: The <small>L</small>-enantiomer of ascorbate and conjugate base of <small>L</small>-ascorbic acid, arising from selective deprotonation of the 3-hydroxy group. Required for a range of essential metabolic reactions in all animals and plants.

Name: cholesteryl linoleate
Definition: The (9Z,12Z)-stereoisomer of cholesteryl octadeca-9,12-dienoate.

Name: D-glucopyranose
Definition: A glucopyranose having <small>D</small>-configuration.

Name: 1,2-dioctadecanoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol where both acyl groups are specified as octadecanoyl.

Name: epitestosterone
Definition: An androstanoid that is the C-17 epimer of testosterone.

Name: hydrogenphosphate
Definition: A phosphate ion that is the conjugate base of dihydrogenphosphate.

Name: 2-O-acetyl-1-O-hexadecyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 2-acetyl-1-alkyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine betaine which has hexadecyl as the alkyl group. PAF is a potent phospholipid activator and mediator of many leukocyte functions, including platelet aggregation, inflammation, and anaphylaxis.

Name: adenosine 5'-monophosphate(2-)
Definition: A nucleoside 5'-monophosphate(2−) that results from the removal of two protons from the phosphate group of adenosine 5'-monophosphate (AMP).

Name: ADP(3-)
Definition: A nucleoside 5'-diphosphate(3−) arising from deprotonation of all three diphosphate OH groups of adenosine 5'-diphosphate (ADP); major species present at pH 7.3.

Name: N-octanoylsphingosine
Definition: An N-acylsphingosine in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as octanoyl.

Name: UTP(4-)
Definition: A nucleoside triphosphate(4−) obtained by global deprotonation of the triphosphate OH groups of UTP; major species present at pH 7.3.

Name: cholesteryl oleate
Definition: The (Z)-stereoisomer of cholesteryl octadec-9-enoate.

Name: L-ornithinium(1+)
Definition: A polar amino acid zwitterion of <small>L</small>-ornithine.

Name: N-acetylsphingosine
Definition: A N-acylsphingosine that has an acetamido group at position 2.

Name: 3-oxo steroid
Definition: Any oxo steroid where an oxo substituent is located at position 3.

Name: (25R)-3alpha,7alpha,12alpha-trihydroxy-5beta-cholestan-26-al
Definition: The (25R)-diastereomer of 3α,7α,12α-trihydroxy-5β-cholestan-26-al.

Name: nickel(2+)
Definition: A nickel cation in which the nickel carries a double positive charge.

Name: tetra-mu3-sulfido-tetrairon
Definition: An Fe<small><sub>4</sub></small>S<small><sub>4</sub></small> iron-sulfur cluster in which each sulfur is attached to three of the iron atoms.

Name: L-cystine residue
Definition: A cystine residue in which both stereocentres have L (= R) configuration.

Name: brexanolone
Definition: A 3-hydroxy-5α-pregnan-20-one in which the hydroxy group at position 3 has α-configuration. It is a metabolite of the sex hormone progesterone and used for the treatment of postpartum depression in women.

Name: taurine zwitterion
Definition: The zwitterion formed from taurine by transfer of a proton from the sulfonyl to the amino group.  It is the major species existing at physiological pH.

Name: 1,2-dioleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as oleoyl.

Name: 2-arachidonoylglycerol
Definition: An endocannabinoid and an endogenous agonist of the cannabinoid receptors (CB1 and CB2). It is an ester formed from omega-6-arachidonic acid and glycerol.

Name: N-acylsphingosine
Definition: The parent compounds of the ceramide family, composed of sphingosine having an unspecified fatty acyl group attached to the nitrogen.

Name: N-tetracosanoylsphinganine
Definition: An N-(1,2-saturated acyl)sphinganine having a tetracosanoyl group attached to the nitrogen atom.

Name: N-hexacosanoylsphinganine
Definition: A dihydroceramide compound having a hexacosanoyl group attached to the nitrogen atom.

Name: 3,3',5-triiodo-L-thyronine zwitterion
Definition: The amino acid zwitterion formed from 3,3',5-triiodo-<small>L</small>-thyronine by tranfer of a proton from the carboxy group to the amino group. It is the major species at pH 7.3.

Name: O-(N-acetyl-alpha-D-galactosaminyl)-L-serine residue
Definition: An L-α-amino acid residue derived from O-(N-acetyl-α-D-galactosaminyl)-L-serine.

Name: triolein
Definition: A triglyceride formed by esterification of the three hydroxy groups of glycerol with oleic acid. Triolein is one of the two components of Lorenzo's oil.

Name: malonaldehyde
Definition: A dialdehyde that is propane substituted by two oxo groups at the terminal carbon atoms respectively. A biomarker of oxidative damage to lipids caused by smoking, it exists in vivo mainly in the enol form.

Name: 2-deoxy-alpha-D-ribose 1-phosphate(2-)
Definition: Dianion of 2-deoxy-α-<small>D</small>-ribose 1-phosphate.

Name: 3,3',5'-triiodo-L-thyronine zwitterion
Definition: Zwitterionic form of 3,3',5'-triiodo-<small>L</small>-thyronine.

Name: 3-sn-phosphatidyl-L-serine(1-)
Definition: A singly-charged anionic phospholipid that is the conjugate base of 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoserine, in which the carboxy and phosphate groups are anionic and the amino group is cationic; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: GDP-beta-L-fucose(2-)
Definition: A nucleotide-sugar oxoanion resulting from the removal of both of the hydrogens from the diphosphate group of GDP-β-<small>L</small>-fucose.

Name: acetoacetyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A 3-oxoacyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of acetoacetyl-CoA.

Name: coenzyme A(4-)
Definition: Tetraanion of coenzyme A.

Name: acetyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of acetyl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups.

Name: glycine zwitterion
Definition: An amino acid zwitterion arising from transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of glycine.

Name: (2S,3S)-3-hydroxy-2-methylbutanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of (2S,3S)-3-hydroxy-2-methylbutanoyl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups.

Name: (S)-3-hydroxybutanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: Tetraanion of (S)-3-hydroxybutanoyl-CoA arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups.

Name: (S)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: The tetraanion of an (S)-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA compound.

Name: trans-dodec-2-enoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: Tetraanion of trans-dodec-2-enoyl-CoA arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: crotonoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: Tetraanion of crotonoyl-CoA arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: 2-methylacetoacetyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of 2-methylacetoacetyl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: isobutyryl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A short chain fatty acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of isobutyryl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: isovaleryl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A short chain fatty acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of isovaleryl-CoA arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: 3-oxopalmitoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of 3-oxopalmitoyl-CoA.

Name: gamma-linolenoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecatrienoyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of γ-linolenoyl-CoA.

Name: arachidonoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of arachidonoyl-CoA.

Name: butyryl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of butyryl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: lauroyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of lauroyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: glutaryl-CoA(5-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA oxoanion that is the pentaanion of glutaryl-CoA arising from deprotonation of phosphate, diphosphate and carboxylic acid functions.

Name: palmitoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A saturated fatty acyl-CoA(4−) that is palmitoyl-CoA in which the phosphate and diphosphate groups have been deprotonated to give the corresponding tetra-anion.

Name: icosanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: Tetraanion of icosanoyl-CoA arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: linoleoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecadienoyl-CoA(4−) resulting from the deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of linoleoyl-CoA.

Name: malonyl-CoA(5-)
Definition: Pentaanion of malonyl-CoA arising from deprotonation of phosphate, diphosphate and carboxylic acid functions.

Name: myristoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA oxoanion that is the tetraanion of myristoyl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: octanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetraanion of octanoyl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: oleoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecenoyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of oleoyl-CoA.

Name: pentanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) that is the tetranion of pentanoyl-CoA, arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions.

Name: phytanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A multi-methyl-branched fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of phytanoyl-CoA .

Name: stearoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4-) arising from deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of stearoyl-CoA.

Name: prostaglandin F2alpha(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin F<small><sub>2α</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: prostaglandin H2(1-)
Definition: Conjugate base of prostaglandin H<small><sub>2</sub></small>.

Name: prostaglandin D2(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin D<small><sub>2</sub></small>., obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 15(S)-HETE(1-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated hydroxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 15(S)-HETE.

Name: 15-oxo-ETE(1-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated oxo fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 15-oxo-ETE.

Name: L-leucine zwitterion
Definition: Zwitterionic form of <small>L</small>-leucine.

Name: thromboxane A2(1-)
Definition: Conjugate base of thromboxane A<small><sub>2</sub></small> arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function.

Name: 15(S)-HPETE(1-)
Definition: Conjugate base of 15(S)-HPETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function.

Name: 5(S)-HPETE(1-)
Definition: Conjugate base of 5(S)-HPETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function.

Name: (6S)-5,6,7,8-tetrahydrofolate(2-)
Definition: Dianion of (6S)-5,6,7,8-tetrahydrofolic acid arising from deprotonation of both carboxylic acid functions.

Name: (6R)-5,10-methenyltetrahydrofolate
Definition: A dicarboxylic acid anion arising from deprotonation of both carboxylic acid functions of (6R)-5,10-methenyltetrahydrofolic acid.

Name: leukotriene A4(1-)
Definition: The leukotriene anion that is the conjugate base of leukotriene A<small><sub>4</sub></small> arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group. Major microspecies at pH 7.3.

Name: L-homoserine zwitterion
Definition: Zwitterionic form of <small>L</small>-homoserine.

Name: 2-oxoadipate(2-)
Definition: Dianion of 2-oxoadipic acid arising from deprotonation of both carboxylic acid groups.

Name: nicotinate D-ribonucleotide(2-)
Definition: Dianion of nicotinic acid <small>D</small>-ribonucleotide arising from deprotonation of carboxylic acid and phosphate functions.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: Dianion of 1-palmitoyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate arising from deprotonation of both OH groups of the phosphate.

Name: O-phosphonato-L-serine(2-)
Definition: An O-phosphonatooxyserine(2−) that is the conjugate base of O-phospho-L-serine.

Name: 1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5,6-tetrakisphosphate(8-)
Definition: Octaanion of 1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5,6-tetrakisphosphate arising from global deprotonation of the acidic phosphate OH groups.

Name: NAD(1-)
Definition: An anionic form of nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide arising from deprotonation of the two OH groups of the diphosphate moiety.

Name: long-chain fatty acid anion
Definition: A fatty acid anion with a chain length of C<small><sub>13</sub></small> to C<small><sub>22</sub></small>.

Name: (R)-2-hydroxyoctadecanoate
Definition: A 2-hydroxyoctadecanoate that has R configuration. The conjugate base of (R)-2-hydroxyoctadecanoic acid.

Name: 2'-deoxycytosine 5'-monophosphate(2-)
Definition: A 2'-deoxyribonucleoside 5'-monophosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 2'-deoxycytidine 5'-monophosphate (dCMP); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: L-histidine zwitterion
Definition: Zwitterionic form of <small>L</small>-histidine having an anionic carboxy group and a protonated α-amino group.

Name: ethanolaminium(1+)
Definition: A primary aliphatic ammonium ion that is the conjugate acid of ethanolamine arising from protonation of the primary amino function.

Name: 3-phosphonato-D-glyceroyl phosphate(4-)
Definition: Tetraanion of 3-phospho-<small>D</small>-glyceroyl dihydrogen phosphate arising from deprotonation of both phosphate groups.

Name: FMNH2(2-)
Definition: Dianion of reduced flavin mononucleotide arising from deprotonation of both phosphate OH groups.

Name: beta-D-fructofuranose 6-phosphate(2-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion that is the dianion of β-<small>D</small>-fructofuranose 6-phosphate arising from deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups.

Name: glycerone phosphate(2-)
Definition: A dianionic form of glycerone phosphate arising from deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: The conjugate base of a 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine compound formed by deprotonation of the phosphate OH group.

Name: chondroitin D-glucuronate anion
Definition: Anionic form of chondroitin <small>D</small>-glucuronate arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid groups of the repeating units; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4-bisphosphate(5-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained via global deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4-bisphosphate.

Name: myo-inositol 1,3,4,6-tetrakisphosphate(8-)
Definition: Octaanion of myo-inositol 1,3,4,6-tetrakisphosphate arising from global deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: dADP(3-)
Definition: A 2'-deoxyribonucleoside 5'-diphosphate obtained by deprotonation of the diphosphate OH groups of 2'-deoxyadenosine 5'-diphosphate (dADP); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3-mercaptopyruvate
Definition: The conjugate base of 3-mercaptopyruvic acid.

Name: FAD(3-)
Definition: Trianion of flavin adenine dinucleotide arising from deprotonation of the diphosphate OH groups and the imide nitrogen.

Name: UDP-N-acetyl-alpha-D-glucosamine(2-)
Definition: Dianion of UDP-N-acetyl-α-D-glucosamine arising from deprotonation of both free diphosphate OH groups; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: alpha-D-ribose 1-phosphate(2-)
Definition: Dianion of α-<small>D</small>-ribose 1-phosphate arising from deprotonation of both phosphate OH groups; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: myo-inositol 1,3,4,5,6-pentakisphosphate(10-)
Definition: The anion obtained from global deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of myo-inositol 1,3,4,5,6-pentakisphosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3',5'-cyclic GMP(1-)
Definition: The conjugate base of guanosine 3',5'-cyclic phosphate arising from deprotonation of the free phosphate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: sphingosine(1+)
Definition: The cationic sphingoid resulting from the protonation of the amino group of sphingosine.

Name: hexanoyl-CoA(4-)

Name: cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline zwitterion

Name: N-(hexanoyl)sphing-4-enine

Name: 1-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion

Name: H group

Name: 1-myristoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine

Name: lysophosphatidylcholine O-16:0/0:0

Name: L-alaniniumyl group

Name: L-glutaminiumyl group
Definition: The organic cationic group formed from <small>L</small>-glutaminium; the major structure at pH 7.3 of the <small>L</small>-glutaminyl group, formed by protonation of the α-amino group.

Name: L-seriniumyl group
