Name: 1-stearoyl-2-palmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A phosphatidylcholine 34:0 in which the two acyl substituents at positions 1 and 2 are specified as stearoyl and palmitoyl respectively.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoserine(1-)
Definition: A phosphatidylserine 34:1 that is the conjugate base of 1-palmitoyl-2-oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-L-serine, in which the carboxy and phosphate groups are anionic and the amino group is cationic.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoserine(1-)
Definition: A phosphatidylserine 34:2 that is the conjugate base of 1-palmitoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-L-serine, in which the carboxy and phosphate groups are anionic and the amino group is cationic.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero3-phosphoserine(1-)
Definition: A phosphatidylserine 36:4 that is the conjugate base of 1-palmitoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-L-serine, in which the carboxy and phosphate groups are anionic and the amino group is cationic.

Name: 1-myristoyl-2-palmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A phosphatidylcholine 30:0 in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are myristoyl (tetradecanoyl) and palmitoyl (hexadecanoyl) respectively.

Name: 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycero 3-phosphocholine in which the acyl group at position 1 is unspecified while that at position 2 is specified as arachidonoyl

Name: 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoserine(1-)
Definition: A 3-sn-phosphatidyl-L-serine(1−) that is the conjugate base of 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-L-serine, in which the carboxy and phosphate groups are anionic and the amino group is cationic.

Name: 1-acyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoserine(1-)
Definition: A 3-sn-phosphatidyl-L-serine(1−) that is the conjugate base of 1-acyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-L-serine, in which the carboxy and phosphate groups are anionic and the amino group is cationic.

Name: 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion in which the acyl group at position 1 is unspecified while that at position 2 is specified as arachidonoyl

Name: 1-acyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion in which the acyl group at position 1 is unspecified while that at position 2 is specified as linoleoyl

Name: 1-oleoylglycerol
Definition: A 1-monoglyceride where the acyl group is oleoyl.

Name: 1-oleoyl-2-stearoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol where oleoyl and stearoyl are the 1- and 2-acyl groups respectively.

Name: 1-oleoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol where oleoyl and arachidonoyl are the 1- and 2-acyl groups respectively.

Name: 1-oleoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol where oleoyl and linoleoyl are the 1- and 2-acyl groups respectively.

Name: 2-stearoylglycerol
Definition: A 2-monoglyceride where the acyl group is octadecanoyl (stearoyl).

Name: 2-linoleoylglycerol
Definition: A 2-monoglyceride where linoleoyl forms the 2-acyl group.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-oleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol with palmitoyl as the 1-acyl group and oleoyl as the 2-acyl group.

Name: 1-octadecanoyl-2-[(9Z)-octadecenoyl]-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol that has stearoyl and oleoyl as the 1- and 2-acyl groups respectively.

Name: 1-myristoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1-acyl-sn-glycerol that is the S-enantiomer of 1-myristoyl glycerol.

Name: 1-hexadecanoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1-acyl-sn-glycerol that has hexadecanoyl (palmitoyl) as the 1-acyl group.

Name: 1-stearoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1-acyl-sn-glycerol that has octadecanoyl (stearoyl) as the 1-acyl group.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specifed as stearoyl and arachidonoyl respectively.

Name: 1,3-dioleoylglycerol
Definition: A 1,3-diglyceride with both acyl groups specified as oleoyl.

Name: 1-oleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1-acyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl group is specified as oleoyl.

Name: 2,3-dioleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 2,3-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the 2- and 3-acyl groups are both specified as oleoyl.

Name: 2-arachidonyl glyceryl ether
Definition: A monoalkylglycerol that is glycerol which is substituted by a (5Z,8Z,11Z,14Z)-icosa-5,8,11,14-tetraen-1-yl group at position 2.

Name: 1-oleoyl-2-O-arachidonyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A monoglyceride that is 1-oleoyl-sn-glycerol in which the hydroxyl hydrogen at position 2 is replaced by an arachidonyl group.

Name: (R)-2-hydroxyhexadecanoate
Definition: The R-enantiomer of 2-hydroxyhexadecanoate.

Name: (R)-2-hydroxylignocerate
Definition: A hydroxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (R)-2-hydroxylignoceric acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group.

Name: 3-oleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 3-acyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl group is (9Z)-octadec-9-enoyl.

Name: dioleoylglycerol
Definition: A diglyceride in which both groups are oleoyl with their positions of acylation unspecified. Formula C<small><sub>39</sub></small>H<small><sub>72</sub></small>O<small><sub>5</sub></small>. For the structure shown, either R1 = H and R2 = oleoyl or R1 = oleoyl and R2 = H.

Name: 2-palmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A lysophosphatidylcholine 16:0 in which the acyl group is specified as palmitoyl (hexadecanoyl) and is located at position 2.

Name: 2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A lysophosphatidylcholine 20:4 in which the acyl group is specified as arachidonoyl and is located at position 2.

Name: oleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine
Definition: A lysophosphatidylcholine 18:1 in which the acyl group is specified as oleoyl and is located at either position 1 or 2.

Name: 2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A 2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion obtained by transfer of a proton from the phosphate to the amino group of 2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (2R)-2-hydroxy fatty acid anion
Definition: A 2-hydroxy fatty acid anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of any (R)-2-hydroxy fatty acid.

Name: riboflavin cyclic 4',5'-phosphate(2-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and imido groups of riboflavin cyclic-4',5'-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (11Z,14Z)-icosadienoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acyl CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of (11Z,14Z)-icosadienoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: dibutyrin
Definition: A diglyceride resulting from the formal condensation of any two of the hydroxy groups of glycerol with the carboxy groups of two molecules of butanoic acid (either R1 = H and R2 = butanoyl, or R1 = butanoyl and R2 = H). A 'closed class'.

Name: (25R)-cholest-5-ene-3beta,26-diol
Definition: A 26-hydroxycholesterol in which the 25-position has R-configuration.

Name: 11,12-EET(1-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 11,12-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 2-hydroxybehenate
Definition: A 2-hydroxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 2-hydroxybehenic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: sphingoid 1-phosphate(1-)
Definition: A generic class of anionic phospholipids, encompassing phosphorylated derivatives of sphinganine, its homologues and stereoisomers, as well as the hydroxy and unsaturated derivatives of these compounds.

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which both the 1- and 2-acyl groups are specified as octanoyl. Formula C19H36O5.

Name: (4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as palmitoyl and arachidonoyl respectively.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as stearoyl and linoleoyl respectively.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-acyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-stearoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-diarachidonoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which both the acyl groups are specified as arachidonoyl.

Name: 1,2-diarachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-acyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1,2-diarachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-dilinoleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which both acyl groups are specified as linoleoyl.

Name: 1,2-dilinoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-acyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1,2-dilinoleoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-(4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as stearoyl and (4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl respectively.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-(4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-stearoyl-2-(4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol 4,5-biphosphate(5-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 4,5-bisphosphate(5−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-1D-myo-inositol 4,5-bisphosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (11Z,14Z)-icosadienoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (11Z,14Z)-icosadienoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (11Z,14Z,17Z)-icosatrienoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (11Z,14Z,17Z)-icosatrienoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosapentaenoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosapentaenoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: pristanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A multi-methyl-branched fatty acyl-CoA(4-) oxanion arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of pristanoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: pristanate
Definition: A methyl-branched fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of pristanic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-(Z)-alk-1-enyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A 1-(alk-1-enyl)-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion in which the alkenyl group has Z geochemistry.

Name: 1-(Z)-alk-1-enyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion
Definition: A 1-(alk-1-enyl)-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine zwitterion in which the alk-1-enyl group has Z geochemistry.

Name: trans-9-octadecenoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecenoyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate functions of trans-9-octadecenoyl-CoA; major species at p 7.3.

Name: 1-hexadecyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-alkyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-hexadecyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: fatty acyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of any fatty acyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 2,2'-lysobisphosphatidate
Definition: A lysobisphosphatidate carrying the fatty acyl groups at positions sn-3 and sn-3' of glycerol.

Name: 1-hexadecyl-2-ammonio-2-deoxy-sn-glycerol
Definition: An organic cation obtained by protonation of the amino group of 1-hexadecyl-2-amino-2-deoxy-sn-glycerol; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-O-palmityl-2-desoxy-2-amino-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(1-)
Definition: An anionic phospholipid that is the conjugate base of 1-O-palmityl-2-desoxy-2-amino-sn-glycero-3-phosphate.

Name: 9(S)-HODE(1-)
Definition: A hydroxy fatty acid anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of 9(S)-HODE; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-[omega-(9R,10R)-epoxy-(13R)-hydroxy-(11E)-octadecenoyloxy]sphing-4-enine

Name: N-[omega-(9R,10R)-epoxy-13-keto-(11E)-octadecenoyloxy]sphing-4-enine

Name: 9(R)-HODE(1-)
Definition: A hydroxy fatty acid anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of 9(R)-HODE; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-oleoylsphingosine
Definition: A N-acylsphingosine in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as oleoyl.

Name: N-dodecanoylphytosphingosine
Definition: A phytoceramide in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as dodecanoyl.

Name: methyl arachidonate
Definition: A fatty acid methyl ester resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of arachidonic acid with methanol.

Name: hepoxilin B3(1-)
Definition: A hepoxilin anion that is the conjugate base of hepoxilin B<small><sub>3</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: trioxilin B3(1-)
Definition: The trioxilin anion that is the anion formed from trioxilin B<small><sub>3</sub></small> by deprotonation of its carboxylic acid moiety; major microspecies present at pH 7.3.

Name: trioxilin A3(1-)
Definition: The trioxilin anion that is the anion formed from trioxilin A<small><sub>3</sub></small> by deprotonation of its carboxylic acid moiety; major microspecies present at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol 3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1-palmityl-2-acetyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-alkyl-2-acyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of 1-palmityl-2-acetyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: ganglioside GD3(2-)

Name: AMP 3'-end(1-) residue

Name: 3'-(L-isoleucyl)adenylyl zwitterionic group

Name: 3'-(L-phenylalanyl)adenylyl(1-) group
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate group of 3'-(<small>L</small>-phenylalanyl)adenylyl group. It is the major structure at pH 7.3 (according to Marvin v 6.2.0.).

Name: 3'-(L-tyrosyl)adenylyl(1-) group
Definition: An organic anionic group obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate group of 3'-(<small>L</small>-tyrosyl)adenylyl group. It is the major structure at pH 7.3 (according to Marvin v 6.2.0.).

Name: ribonucleoside 2'-monophosphate(2-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of any ribonucleoside 2'-monophosphate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5alpha-pregan-20beta-ol-3-one
Definition: A 3-oxo-5α- steroid that is 5α-pregane-3-one carrying an additional hydroxy substituent at the 20β-position.

Name: 21-dehydrocortisol
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is cortisol in which the 21-hydroxy group has been oxidised to the corresponding aldehyde.

Name: 21-dehydrocortisone
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is cortisone in which the 21-hydroxy group has been oxidised to the corresponding aldehyde.

Name: 11-dehydrocorticosterone
Definition: An 11-oxo steroid that is corticosterone in which the hydroxy substituent at the 11β position has been oxidised to give the corresponding ketone.

Name: 11,21-dioxoprogesterone
Definition: A C<small><sub>21</sub></small>-steroid that is progesterone carrying two additional oxo substituents at positions 11 and 21

Name: isoursodeoxycholate
Definition: A steroid acid anion that is the conjugate base of isoursodeoxycholic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3beta-hydroxy-7-oxo-5beta-cholan-24-oate
Definition: A steroid acid anion that is the conjugate base of 3β-hydroxy-7-oxo-5β-cholan-24-oic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: ursodeoxycholate
Definition: A bile acid anion that is the conjugate base of ursodeoxycholic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 7-oxolithocholate
Definition: A bile acid anion that is the conjugate base of 7-oxolithocholic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3-hydroxybutanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of 3-hydroxybutanoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (5Z,8Z,11Z,13E)-15-HETE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 15-HETE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 11(R)-HETE(1-)
Definition: An 11-HETE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 11(R)-HETE(1−), obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 15(R)-HETE(1-)
Definition: A 15-HETE(1−) that is the conjugate base of 15(R)-HETE(1−), obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: hexadecanoate
Definition: A long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of hexadecanoic acid (palmitic acid); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: tricosanoate
Definition: A very long-chain fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of tricosanoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosyl-(1<->1)-ceramide(1-)

Name: alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->8)-alpha-N-acetylneuraminyl-(2->3)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosyl-(1<->1)-ceramide(2-)

Name: 3,17-dioxoandrost-4-en-19-al
Definition: An androstanoid that is androst-4-en-19-al substituted by oxo groups at positions 3 and 17.

Name: 15(R)-HPETE(1-)
Definition: A hydroperoxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 15(R)-HPETE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 11(R)-HPETE(1-)
Definition: A hydroperoxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 11(R)-HPETE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: prostaglandin G2(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin G<small><sub>2</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycerol 3-diphosphate(3-)
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol 3-diphosphate(3−) arising from deprotonation of the diphosphate OH groups of 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycerol 3-diphosphate.

Name: N-palmitoylhexadecasphinganine
Definition: An N-acylhexadecasphinganine in which the acyl group is specified as palmitoyl (hexadecanoyl).

Name: N-stearoylhexadecasphinganine
Definition: An N-acylhexadecasphinganine in which the acyl group is specified as stearoyl (octadecanoyl).

Name: 1,2-dioctadecanoyl-sn-glycerol-3-phosphate(2-)
Definition: A 1-acyl-2-octadecanoyl-sn-glycerol-3-phosphate(2−) in which the 1-acyl group is also octadecanoyl; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-di[(4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-linoleoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the 1- and 2-acyl groups are specified as palmitoyl and linoleoyl respectively.

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-[(4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl]-sn-glycero-3-phosphate(2-)

Name: 1,2-dipalmitoyl-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which both acyl groups are specified as palmitoyl (hexadecanoyl).

Name: 1-palmitoyl-2-[(4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl]-sn-glycerol
Definition: A 1,2-diacyl-sn-glycerol in which the acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as palmitoyl and (4Z,7Z,10Z,13Z,16Z,19Z)-docosahexaenoyl respectively.

Name: 17beta-estradiol 17-glucosiduronate
Definition: A monocarboxylic acid anion resulting from the deprotonation of the carboxy group of 17β-estradiol 17-glucosiduronic acid.

Name: glycodeoxycholate
Definition: A N-acylglycinate that is the conjugate base of glycodeoxycholic acid.

Name: linoleamide
Definition: A fatty amide obtained from  linoleic acid.

Name: (R)-lipoate
Definition: A lipoate that is the conjugate base of (R)-lipoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N(6)-[(R)-lipoyl]-L-lysine residue
Definition: An N6-lipoyl-L-lysine residue in which the lipoyl group has R-configuration.

Name: N(6)-[(R)-dihydrolipoyl]-L-lysine residue
Definition: An N6-dihydrolipoyl-L-lysine residue in which the dihydrolipoyl group has R-configuration.

Name: N(6)-[(R)-S(8)-acetyldihydrolipoyl]-L-lysine residue
Definition: An N6-(S8-acetyldihydrolipoyl)-L-lysine residue in which the dihydrolipoyl group has R-configuration.

Name: long-chain fatty acyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A fatty acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of any long-chain fatty acyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N(6)-[(R)-S(8)-isobutyryldihydrolipoyl]-L-lysine residue
Definition: An α-amino-acid residue derived from N6-[(R)-S8-isobutyryldihydrolipoyl]-L-lysine.

Name: glycino(1-) group
Definition: An α-amino-acid residue anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of glycino group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: L-alpha-amino acid residue
Definition: An α-amino-acid residue derived from an <small>L</small>-α-amino acid.

Name: 1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5-triphosphate)(7-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5-trisphosphate(7−) in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are specified as stearoyl and arachidonoyl respectively.

Name: N-(2-hydroxyhexanoyl)sphingosine
Definition: A 2-N-(2-hydroxyacyl)sphingosine in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as 2-hydroxyhexanoyl.

Name: N-(2-hydroxyhexanoyl)-beta-D-galactosylsphingosine
Definition: A N-(2-hydroxyacyl)-β-D-galactosylsphingosine in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as 2-hydroxyhexanoyl.

Name: N-(2-hydroxyhexanoyl)sphinganine
Definition: An N-(2-hydroxyacyl)sphinganine in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as 2-hydroxyhexanoyl.

Name: N-(2-hydroxyhexanoyl)-beta-D-galactosylsphinganine
Definition: A N-(2-hydroxyacyl)-β-D-galactosylsphinganine in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as 2-hydroxyhexanoyl.

Name: N-acylsphingoid
Definition: A ceramide consisting of an undefined sphingoid base linked to an undefined fatty acid via an amide bond.

Name: L-citrulline residue
Definition: An <small>L</small>-α-amino acid residue derived from <small>L</small>-citrulline.

Name: alkyl sulfate(1-)
Definition: An organosulfate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the sulfo group of any alkyl sulfate; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1D-myo-inositol-3,4,5-trisphosphate)(7-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5-trisphosphate(7−) in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are both specified as octanoyl.

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1D-myo-inositol-3,4-bisphosphate)(5-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4-bisphosphate(5−) in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are both specified as octanoyl.

Name: 1,2-dioctanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1D-myo-inositol-4,5-bisphosphate)(5-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 4,5-bisphosphate(5−) in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are both specified as octanoyl.

Name: 1,2-dihexadecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1D-myo-inositol-3,4,5-trisphosphate)(7-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4,5-trisphosphate(7−) in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are both specified as hexadecanoyl (palmitoyl).

Name: O-phospho-L-serine(2-) residue
Definition: An α-amino-acid residue anion obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate OH groups of O-phospho-L-serine residue; major speices at pH 7.3.

Name: 1,2-dihexadecanoyl-sn-glycero-3-phospho-(1D-myo-inositol-3,4-bisphosphate)(5-)
Definition: A 1-phosphatidyl-1D-myo-inositol 3,4-bisphosphate(5−) in which the phosphatidyl acyl groups at positions 1 and 2 are both specified as hexadecanoyl (palmitoyl).

Name: sphinga-4E,14Z-dienine(1+)
Definition: A cationic sphingoid obtained by the protonation of the amino group of sphinga-4E,14Z-dienine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-terminal N-acetyl-L-alanine residue
Definition: An N-terminal α-amino-acid residue derived from N-terminal N-acetyl-L-alanine.

Name: N-terminal N-acetyl-L-serine residue
Definition: An N-terminal α-amino-acid residue derived from N-terminal N-acetyl-L-serine.

Name: medium-chain 3-substituted propanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A 3-substituted propionyl-CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of any medium-chain 3-substituted propionyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: medium-chain 2,3-trans-enoyl CoA(4-)
Definition: A 2,3-trans-enoyl CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of any medium-chain 2,3-trans-enoyl CoA (medium-chain = chain length between C6 and C12); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: peptidylproline (omega=0) residue
Definition: The organodiyl group derived from peptidylproline (ω=0).

Name: peptidylproline (omega=180) residue
Definition: The organodiyl group derived from peptidylproline (ω=180)

Name: 2-saturated fatty acid anion
Definition: A fatty acid anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group of any 2-saturated fatty acid.

Name: beta-D-glucosylsphingosine(1+)
Definition: A <small>D</small>-glucosylsphingosine(1+) obtained by protonation of the amino group of any β-<small>D</small>-glucosylsphingosine; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 9(10)-EpOME(1-)
Definition: A monounsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 9(10)-EpOME, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 14,15-EET(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 14,15-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 8,9-EET(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 8,9-EET, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 9,10-DiHOME(1-)
Definition: A monounsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 9,10-DiHOME, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (5Z,8Z,11Z)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (5Z,8Z,11Z)-14,15-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (5Z,8Z,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (5Z,8Z,14Z)-11,12-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (5Z,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (5Z,11Z,14Z)-8,9-dihydroxyicosatrienoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 12-hydroxyoctadecanoate
Definition: A hydroxy saturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 12-hydroxyoctadecanoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: sphingoid base(1+)
Definition: A cationic sphingoid obtained by protonation of the amino group of any 2-amino-1,3-dihydroxysphingoid base.

Name: 4-oxo-L-proline zwitterion
Definition: An amino acid zwitterion resulting from a transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of 4-oxo-<small>L</small>-proline; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: aromatic L-alpha-amino acid zwitterion
Definition: An <small>L</small>-α-amino acid zwitterion resulting from a transfer of a proton from the carboxy to the amino group of any aromatic <small>L</small>-α-amino acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 12-(phosphonooxy)octadecanoate(3-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy and phosphate OH groups of 12-(phosphonooxy)octadecanoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (9E)-12-(phosphonooxy)octadecenoate(3-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy and phosphate OH groups of 12-phosphonooxy-(9E)-octadecenoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (9Z)-12-(phosphonooxy)octadecenoate(3-)
Definition: An organophosphate oxoanion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy and phosphate OH groups of (9Z)-12-(phosphonooxy)octadecenoic acid; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (9Z)-12-hydroxyoctadec-9-enoate
Definition: A hydroxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (9Z)-12-hydroxyoctadec-9-enoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (9E)-12-hydroxyoctadec-9-enoate
Definition: A hydroxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (9E)-12-hydroxyoctadec-9-enoic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: prostaglandin G2 2-glyceryl ester
Definition: A 2-monoglyceride obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin G<small><sub>2</sub></small> with the 2-hydroxy group of glycerol.

Name: prostaglandin H2 2-glyceryl ester
Definition: A 2-monoglyceride obtained by formal condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin H<small><sub>2</sub></small> with the 2-hydroxy group of glycerol.

Name: 15-deoxy-Delta(12,14)-prostaglandin J2(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of 15-deoxy-Δ<small><sup>12,14</small></sup>-prostaglandin J<small><sub>2</sub></small>. obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N-dodecanoylsphinganine
Definition: A dihydroceramide in which the ceramide N-acyl group is specified as dodecanoyl (lauroyl).

Name: N-(dodecanoyl)ethanolamine
Definition: An N-(long-chain-acyl)ethanolamine resulting from the formal condensation of the carboxy group of dodecanoic acid (myristic acid) with the amino group of ethanolamine.

Name: N-(octadecanoyl)ethanolamine
Definition: An N-acylethanolamine 18:0 that is the ethanolamide of octadecanoic acid.

Name: L-3-oxoalanine residue
Definition: An <small>L</small>-α-amino acid residue derived from <small>L</small>-3-oxoalanine.

Name: (11E,13Z)-octadecadienoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecadienoyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of (11E,13Z)-octadecadienoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (5Z,11E)-octadecadienoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecadienoyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of (5Z,11E)-octadecadienoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (6Z,11E)-octadecadienoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An octadecadienoyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of (6Z,11E)-octadecadienoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 4beta-hydroxycholesterol
Definition: A oxysterol that is cholesterol in which the hydrogen at the 4β position has been replaced by a hydroxy group. A metabolite of cholesterol formed by the drug-metabolizing enzyme cytochrome P450 3A4, it is one of the major oxysterols in the human circulation.

Name: (25R)-3beta-hydroxycholest-5-en-26-al
Definition: A 3β-hydroxycholest-5-en-26-al in which the stereocentre at position 25 has R-configuration.

Name: (25R)-3beta-hydroxycholest-5-en-26-oate
Definition: A 3β-hydroxycholest-5-en-26-oate in which the stereocentre at position 25 has R-configuration.

Name: (25R)-4beta,26-dihydroxycholesterol
Definition: An oxysterol that is cholesterol which is substituted by hydroxy groups at 4β and 26 positions and has R-configuration at position 25.

Name: (25R)-3beta,4beta-dihydroxycholest-5-en-26-al
Definition: A 26-oxo steroid resulting from the oxidation of the terminal methyl group of 4β,26-dihydroxycholesterol to the corresponding aldehyde.

Name: (25R)-3beta,4beta-dihydroxycholest-5-en-26-oate(1-)
Definition: A steroid acid anion that is the conjugate base of (25R)-3β,4β-dihydroxycholest-5-en-26-oic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (2E)-pentenoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A 2,3-trans-enoyl CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of (2E)-pentenoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5beta-dihydroepitestosterone
Definition: An androstanoid with a 5β-configuration formed by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond of epitestosterone.

Name: 5beta-dihydrocorticosterone
Definition: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid formed from corticosterone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

Name: 5beta-dihydrodeoxycorticosterone
Definition: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid formed from 11-deoxycorticosterone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

Name: 5beta-dihydroaldosterone
Definition: A 3-oxo-5β-steroid formed from aldosterone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

Name: (5alpha)-cholestan-3beta-ol
Definition: A cholestanoid that is (5α)-cholestane substituted by a β-hydroxy group at position 3.

Name: O-(N-acetyl-alpha-D-galactosaminyl)-L-threonine residue
Definition: An L-α-amino acid residue derived from O-(N-acetyl-α-D-galactosaminyl)-L-threonine.

Name: N-terminal 5-oxo-L-proline residue
Definition: An N-terminal α-amino-acid residue derived from 5-oxo-<small>L</small>-proline.

Name: 17beta-hydroxy-5beta-androst-1-en-3-one
Definition: A 3-oxo Δ<small><sup>1</small></sup>-steroid with 5β-configuration formed from 17β-hydroxyandrosta-1,4-dien-3-one by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

Name: 17beta-hydroxy-5beta-estran-3-one
Definition: A C18 steroid with 5β-configuration formed from nandrolone by reduction across the C4-C5 double bond.

Name: (25R)-3beta,26-dihydroxycholest-5-en-7-one
Definition: An oxysterol that is 7-ketocholesterol which is substituted by a hydroxy group at position 26 and has R-configuration at position 25.

Name: (25R)-3beta-hydroxycholest-5-en-7-one-26-al
Definition: A 26-oxo steroid resulting from the oxidation of (25R)-3β,26-dihydroxycholest-5-en-7-one to the corresponding aldehyde.

Name: (25R)-3beta-hydroxycholest-5-en-7-one-26-oate
Definition: A steroid acid anion that is the conjugate base of (25R)-3β-hydroxycholest-5-en-7-one-26-oic acid, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: alpha-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-galactosyl-(1->4)-beta-D-glucosyl-(1<->1)-ceramide
Definition: A glycotriaosylceramide having α-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-<small>D</small>-galactosyl-(1→4)-β-<small>D</small>-glucosyl component attached to the primary hydroxy function of a ceramide with undefined sphingoid base.

Name: (S)-2-methylbutanoyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A 2-methylbutanoyl-CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of phosphate and diphosphate functions of (S)-2-methylbutanoyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: N(omega),N('omega)-dimethyl-L-arginine(1+) residue
Definition: An α-amino-acid residue cation obtained by protonation of the guanidino group of Nω,N'ω-dimethyl-L-arginine residue.

Name: prostaglandin E2 1-glyceryl ester
Definition: A 1-monoglyceride resulting from the condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin E<small><sub>2</sub></small> with the 1-hydroxy group of glycerol.

Name: prostaglandin F2alpha 1-glyceryl ester
Definition: A 1-monoglyceride resulting from the condensation of the carboxy group of prostaglandin F<small><sub>2α</sub></small> with the 1-hydroxy group of glycerol.

Name: L-beta-isoaspartate residue
Definition: An anionic amino-acid residue formed following deamidation of <small>L</small>-asparagine or dehydration of <small>L</small>-aspartate residue.

Name: L-isoaspartate alpha-methyl ester residue
Definition: An amino acid residue derived from <small>L</small>-isoaspartic acid α-methyl ester.

Name: 5(S)-HETE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 5(S)-HETE, obtained by deprotonation of the hydroxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 12(S)-HHTrE(1-)
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 12(S)-HHTrE, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 12-HETE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 12-HETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: very long-chain 3-oxoacyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: A 3-oxoacyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of any very long-chain 3-oxoacyl-CoA (very long-chain = chain length greater than C<small><sub>22</sub></small>); major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 3-oxoacyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) arising from deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of any 3-oxoacyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: very long-chain acyl-CoA(4-)
Definition: An acyl-CoA(4−) obtained by deprotonation of the phosphate and diphosphate OH groups of a very-long-chain acyl-CoA; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: S-substituted glutathione(1-)
Definition: A peptide anion obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy groups and concomitant protonation of the amino group of any S-substituted glutathione. Major structure at pH 7.3

Name: prostaglandin H1(1-)
Definition: A prostaglandin carboxylic acid anion that is the conjugate base of prostaglandin H<small><sub>1</sub></small>, obtained by deprotonation of the carboxy group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5(S),11(R)-DiHETE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 5(S),11(R)-DiHETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5(S),15(R)-DiHETE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 5(S),15(R)-DiHETE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 5(S),15(S)-DiHETE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 5(S),15(S)-DiHETE, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 17(R)-HDoHE(1-)
Definition: A hydroxy polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 17(R)-HDoHE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (4Z,7Z,10Z,14E,16Z,19Z)-13-hydroxydocosahexaenoate
Definition: A hydroxy polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 13-HDoHE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 18(R)-HEPE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 18(R)-HEPE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 15(R)-HEPE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 15(R)-HEPE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: 11(R)-HEPE(1-)
Definition: An icosanoid anion that is the conjugate base of 11(R)-HEPE arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: (7Z,10Z,13R,14E,16Z,19Z)-13-hydroxydocosapentaenoate
Definition: A polyunsaturated fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of (13R)-hydroxy-(7Z,10Z,14E,16Z,19Z)-docosapentaenoic acid, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid group; major species at pH 7.3. It is an intermediate in 13-series resolvins biosynthesis from DPA (ω-3).

Name: O-(N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosaminyl)-L-serine residue
Definition: The L-α-amino acid residue formed from O-(N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl)-L-serine.

Name: O-(N-acetyl-beta-D-glucosaminyl)-L-threonine residue
Definition: The L-α-amino acid residue formed from O-(N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminyl)-L-threonine.

Name: 13(S)-HODE(1-)
Definition: A hydroxy fatty acid anion that is the conjugate base of 13(S)-HODE, arising from deprotonation of the carboxylic acid function; major species at pH 7.3.

Name: taurochenodeoxycholate
Definition: An organosulfonate oxoanion that is the conjugate base of taurochenodeoxycholic acid arising from deprotonation of the sulfonate OH group; major species at pH 7.3.
